Official Reference Wiki

JunkRats Wiki

Documentation for the world, characters, locations, creatures, and development of JunkRats.

Browse Lore 🚧 Under Construction This wiki is currently being expanded. Certain pages, images, and lore entries may be incomplete or unavailable while documentation is being developed.

This wiki is the main reference source for JunkRats and related world information.

docs.title: Characters Index
docs.text:
The Characters section documents individuals who appear within the JunkRats setting.

These pages describe notable survivors, leaders, and other figures connected to the world following The Collapse. Character articles typically include background information, affiliations, roles in the story, and relationships with other characters, locations, or factions.

docs.title: Main Characters
docs.text:
These individuals form the primary group followed throughout the JunkRats narrative.

Adrian

Leader of the JunkRats and a former Runner from the Underground.

Lucy

Protector of the JunkRats and an experienced Runner.

Gabriel

Technical specialist responsible for operating and restoring abandoned systems.

Zariah

Weapons specialist who joins the group after surviving independently on the surface.

docs.title: Supporting Characters
docs.text:
Supporting characters appear throughout the story or play roles in the broader world of the JunkRats setting.

Vince

Leader of the Underground and one of the architects of the crater settlement.

Michael

An individual connected to events surrounding the JunkRats.

docs.title: Character Roles
docs.text:
Characters within the JunkRats setting generally fall into several categories.

Runners Individuals sent from The Underground to recover resources and technology from The Surface.

Underground Residents Members of the crater settlement responsible for maintaining infrastructure, security, and daily operations.

Surface Survivors Individuals who live or operate outside the settlement in the ruins of the city.

Independent / Unknown Characters whose affiliations or loyalties are unclear.

docs.title: Adrian
docs.text: Adrian Profile Role Runner / Leader of the JunkRats Occupation Surface scavenger Affiliation The Underground Personal Age 27 Gender Male Status Alive Additional Information Voice Actor N/A

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Adrian is a central character in the JunkRats narrative. A survivor from The Underground and an active Runner, he later becomes the leader of the group known as the JunkRats.

Adrian participates in surface expeditions across The Surface, recovering supplies and materials from the ruins of the former city while encountering Forsaken creatures and other hazards present outside the crater settlement.

docs.title: Background
docs.text:
Adrian was sixteen years old during The Collapse, when the spread of the Forsaken Virus led to the collapse of human civilization. Following the outbreak, he became one of the survivors living within The Underground, a settlement established inside a massive crater beneath the ruined city.

Life inside the Underground relied on strict resource control and organized scavenging operations. Citizens selected for the Runner program were sent to the surface to recover materials needed to sustain the settlement.

As Adrian gained experience operating outside the crater, he became familiar with the dangers of the ruined city, including unstable infrastructure and infected territory.

docs.title: Story
docs.text:
Adrian is one of the central protagonists of the JunkRats campaign and the leader of the group later known as the JunkRats.

Adrian was raised in The Underground following the global collapse caused by the Forsaken Virus. As an adult he was selected for the Runner program, a system in which citizens were assigned to surface scavenging missions to recover scrap, technology, and other resources required to sustain the Underground settlement.

During his time operating on the surface, Adrian began secretly establishing a refuge in Dead Man’s Land. The location was an abandoned corner store several miles from the crater that he converted into a hidden safehouse between supply runs.

While working as a Runner, Adrian eventually formed a partnership with Lucy. The two began conducting surface expeditions together and later encountered Gabriel, a technician capable of bypassing sealed infrastructure and restoring electrical systems. Gabriel later joined the group and began maintaining the equipment and power systems inside the safehouse.

The group expanded further when they encountered Zariah, a survivor scavenging independently in the ruins who specialized in building and modifying weapons from scrap components. With her addition, the four began operating together regularly on the surface.

Over time the group began operating outside the authority of The Underground. Their activities included maintaining an independent shelter on the surface and diverting some recovered materials to sustain their safehouse.

These actions were eventually discovered by Underground scouts. Adrian, Lucy, Gabriel, and Zariah were arrested and charged with treason, including accusations of resource diversion and unauthorized operations outside Underground control.

The four were transported to an abandoned subway platform used for executions and prepared for immediate execution. Before the sentence could be carried out, a structural collapse caused by Forsaken growths destabilizing the surrounding tunnels destroyed part of the platform. The collapse killed the execution officer and allowed Adrian to free the others, enabling their escape to the surface and initiating the events of the JunkRats campaign.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Adrian is known for maintaining a calm and practical approach during dangerous situations. His experience operating on the surface has made him cautious, observant, and capable of making decisions under pressure.

Within the group, Adrian frequently assumes a leadership role, organizing movement during expeditions and directing the team's response to threats encountered outside the crater.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
Adrian regularly operates alongside several individuals:

  • Lucy
  • Gabriel
  • Zariah

Together they form the group later known as the JunkRats. Their relationship develops through repeated surface expeditions and their shared conflict with the authority of The Underground.

docs.title: Gabriel
docs.text: Gabriel Profile Role Runner / Technical Specialist of the JunkRats Occupation Surface scavenger Affiliation The Underground Personal Age 26 Gender Male Status Alive Additional Information Voice Actor N/A

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Gabriel is one of the central characters in the JunkRats narrative. A survivor from The Underground and an active Runner, he later becomes a core member of the group known as the JunkRats.

Gabriel participates in expeditions across The Surface, assisting the group with technical knowledge and the operation of equipment recovered from the ruins of the former city while encountering the ongoing threat posed by the Forsaken.

docs.title: Background
docs.text:
Gabriel lived within The Underground, the crater settlement formed after the devastation of The Collapse and the spread of the Forsaken Virus.

Like other members of the Runner program, Gabriel was assigned to travel to the The Surface to recover materials and technology from the abandoned city above. These missions exposed him to the dangers of the infected world and the deteriorating infrastructure left behind after the collapse of civilization.

Through repeated exposure to damaged systems and abandoned facilities, Gabriel developed familiarity with electronic equipment, sealed infrastructure, and power systems found throughout the ruins.

docs.title: Story
docs.text:
Gabriel is a major participant in the events of the JunkRats campaign and serves as the primary technical specialist of the group later known as the JunkRats.

Before joining the team, Gabriel operated independently on the surface while scavenging technology and bypassing sealed infrastructure left behind in the ruined city. His technical skills allowed him to manipulate electronic locks, restore power systems, and access locations that many other scavengers could not reach.

Gabriel first encountered Adrian and Lucy during a surface expedition at a sealed maintenance facility. While the pair attempted to access the structure, Gabriel successfully bypassed the electronic lock shortly before a group of Forsaken creatures attacked the entrance. The three survivors retreated inside the facility after the door sealed behind them.

Following the encounter, Adrian invited Gabriel to relocate to a hidden safehouse located in Dead Man’s Land. Gabriel accepted the offer and began maintaining the location’s electrical systems, wiring, and equipment. His technical expertise allowed the group to maintain working power and repair scavenged devices used during surface expeditions.

While operating together, Gabriel worked closely with Adrian and Lucy during supply runs conducted for The Underground. The group later encountered Zariah, a scavenger specializing in weapon construction and modification. With her addition, the four began operating regularly as a team on the surface.

As the group continued operating outside the direct authority of The Underground, their activities eventually attracted the attention of Underground scouts. Gabriel, along with Adrian, Lucy, and Zariah, was arrested and charged with treason for diverting resources and conducting unauthorized operations on the surface.

The four were transported to an abandoned subway platform used for executions. Before the sentence could be carried out, structural damage caused by Forsaken growth destabilizing the surrounding tunnels triggered a collapse that destroyed part of the platform. The collapse killed the execution officer and allowed Adrian to free the others, enabling Gabriel and the rest of the group to escape to the surface.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Gabriel is known for his direct and often blunt manner of speaking. His communication style can create tension within the group during stressful situations.

Despite this, his technical knowledge and ability to operate damaged systems make him an important contributor during surface expeditions. Gabriel frequently handles electronic equipment, power systems, and sealed infrastructure encountered in the ruins.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
Gabriel regularly operates alongside several individuals:

  • Adrian
  • Lucy
  • Zariah

Together they form the group later known as the JunkRats. Their relationship develops through repeated expeditions on the surface and their shared conflict with the authority of The Underground.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text: • Adrian

docs.title: Lucy
docs.text: Lucy Profile Role Runner / Protector of the JunkRats Occupation Surface scavenger Affiliation The Underground Personal Age 25 Gender Female Status Alive Additional Information Voice Actor N/A

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Lucy is a human survivor and a member of the Runners operating under The Underground. She is one of the four central characters in the JunkRats narrative and a founding member of the group later known as the JunkRats.

Lucy participates in surface expeditions across The Surface, recovering resources from the ruins of the former city while confronting Forsaken creatures and other hazards present outside the crater settlement.

docs.title: Background
docs.text:
Lucy lives within The Underground, a settlement located inside the massive crater created during early attempts to contain the spread of the Forsaken Virus following The Collapse.

Like other members of the Runner program, Lucy is assigned to travel to the The Surface to recover scrap, equipment, and other materials needed to sustain the settlement. These missions expose her to the ruins of the former city as well as the infected creatures that inhabit it.

Lucy becomes one of the more experienced individuals operating on the surface due to repeated participation in Runner expeditions.

docs.title: Story
docs.text:
Lucy is a major participant in the events of the JunkRats campaign and a long-time partner of Adrian during their time as Runners operating on the surface.

The two first began working together during Runner supply missions conducted by The Underground. Lucy quickly demonstrated strong combat ability during encounters with the Forsaken, and the two began conducting missions together regularly.

During this period Adrian revealed the existence of a hidden refuge in Dead Man’s Land, an abandoned corner store converted into a safehouse between supply runs. Lucy became the first person Adrian allowed inside the location and later assisted in maintaining the shelter while continuing to carry out missions for the Underground.

Lucy and Adrian later encountered Gabriel during a surface expedition at a sealed maintenance facility. After assisting them in bypassing the locked entrance and surviving a Forsaken attack, Gabriel joined the group and began maintaining the safehouse’s electrical systems and equipment.

The group expanded further when they encountered Zariah, an independent scavenger specializing in weapon construction and modification. With her addition, the four began operating together regularly on the surface.

Their continued activity outside the direct authority of The Underground eventually attracted the attention of Underground scouts. Lucy, along with Adrian, Gabriel, and Zariah, was arrested and charged with treason for diverting supplies and conducting unauthorized operations on the surface.

The four were transported to an abandoned subway platform used for executions. Before the sentence could be carried out, structural damage caused by Forsaken growth destabilizing the surrounding tunnels triggered a collapse that destroyed part of the platform. The collapse allowed the group to escape to the surface, initiating the events that lead to the formation of the JunkRats.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Lucy is known for her strong protective instincts toward members of the group. During expeditions she frequently prioritizes the safety of others and is often among the first to react when a situation becomes dangerous.

She is also known for questioning decisions that may place the group at unnecessary risk, particularly during high-pressure situations encountered on the surface.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
Lucy regularly operates alongside several other individuals:

  • Adrian
  • Gabriel
  • Zariah

Together they form the group later known as the JunkRats. Their relationship develops through repeated surface expeditions and shared conflict with the authority of The Underground.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Adrian
  • Gabriel

docs.title: Michael
docs.text: Michael Profile Role Right-Hand Man of Vince Occupation Scientist / Researcher Affiliation The Underground Personal Age 34 Gender Male Status Unknown Additional Information Voice Actor N/A

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Michael is a scientist and senior figure within The Underground. Known for his analytical mindset and interest in the biology of the Forsaken, he becomes closely associated with research efforts related to the Forsaken Virus.

Within the leadership structure of the Underground, Michael operates as a trusted advisor and right-hand man to Vince.

docs.title: Background
docs.text:
Michael arrived in the Underground as a survivor during the early years following The Collapse. Witnesses described him as a hardened individual who appeared to have survived direct encounters with the Forsaken before reaching the settlement.

After his arrival, Michael rarely appeared among the general population. Rumors circulated within the settlement that Vince had taken a particular interest in him and kept him working on specialized projects.

Over time, Michael developed a reputation for being methodical and highly analytical. Unlike many residents of the Underground who feared the Forsaken, Michael viewed the infection as something that could be studied and potentially understood.

He believed that simply avoiding the Forsaken was not a permanent solution and that long-term survival would require humanity to gain leverage over the virus itself.

docs.title: Role in the Underground
docs.text:
Within the leadership structure of The Underground, Michael served as one of Vince’s most dependable subordinates.

Where Vince focused on maintaining order and stability within the settlement, Michael concentrated on understanding the biological threat posed by the Forsaken. His work centered around researching the virus and analyzing how it affects infected organisms.

Although the official policy of the Underground emphasized containment and avoidance of the virus, Michael privately pursued deeper research into its properties, believing that mastery of the infection could secure the settlement’s future.

docs.title: Story
docs.text:
Michael appears during the events of the JunkRats campaign as part of the leadership structure within The Underground. His actions and decisions influence several events that affect Adrian, Lucy, Gabriel, and Zariah as the story progresses.

Act I Spoilers

Michael secretly conducts unauthorized experiments involving the Forsaken Virus on captured subjects. During one of these experiments he infects Adrian, Lucy, Gabriel, and Zariah with a modified strain of the virus.

The experiment produces unexpected results when the infection stabilizes rather than causing full mutation.

After recording the results, Michael attempts to eliminate the subjects and secure the data.

Act II Spoilers

Michael later presents the results of his research to Vince. Vince accuses him of endangering The Underground by experimenting on citizens without authorization.

Vince executes Michael in his office shortly afterward.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Michael is portrayed as calm, precise, and intensely analytical. He rarely acts impulsively and prefers to evaluate situations based on measurable outcomes.

His fascination with the Forsaken stems from a belief that understanding the virus is the only path to securing humanity’s survival.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
Michael is closely connected to several major figures in the JunkRats narrative.

  • Vince — Leader of The Underground and Michael’s direct superior
  • Adrian
  • Lucy
  • Gabriel
  • Zariah

His actions during the campaign directly influence the events that lead to the formation of the JunkRats.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Adrian
  • Lucy
  • Gabriel

docs.title: Vince
docs.text: Vince Profile Role Leader of The Underground Occupation Settlement leader Affiliation The Underground Personal Age 45 Gender Male Status Unknown Additional Information Voice Actor N/A

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Vince is the leader of The Underground, the survivor settlement located within the massive crater formed during early attempts to contain the spread of the Forsaken Virus after The Collapse.

He is one of the primary figures responsible for organizing the crater settlement and establishing the systems that allow the community to survive beneath the ruins of the former city.

docs.title: Background
docs.text:
Following The Collapse and the rapid spread of the Forsaken Virus, large portions of the city became uninhabitable. Survivors fled into subway tunnels, service corridors, and maintenance routes beneath the city in an attempt to avoid the infected surface.

During this period, Vince and a small group of volunteers began mapping the underground transit routes in search of secure areas where survivors could regroup. While exploring deeper sections of the tunnel network, they eventually discovered the massive crater created by an early containment bombing.

The crater provided a defensible location separated from the heavily infected ruins above. Over time, survivors began settling within the area, forming what would later become known as The Underground.

Vince became one of the central figures responsible for organizing the growing settlement and coordinating its survival efforts.

docs.title: Role in the Settlement
docs.text:
As the population within the crater increased, Vince assumed a leadership role in maintaining order and coordinating survival operations.

Under his leadership, the settlement established structured systems for resource management, security, and exploration of the surface. One of the most important systems created during this period was the formation of the Runners, individuals tasked with traveling to the The Surface to recover supplies, scrap, and technology from the abandoned city.

These expeditions became essential to sustaining life within the Underground.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Vince is generally portrayed as pragmatic and authoritative. The conditions following The Collapse required decisions that prioritized the long-term stability of the settlement.

While his leadership contributed to the survival of The Underground, some of the policies implemented under his authority have created tension with those assigned to perform the most dangerous tasks on the surface.

docs.title: Leadership
docs.text: Vince oversees the governing structure of The Underground, including resource allocation, security operations, and Runner expeditions.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
As the leader of The Underground, Vince oversees the systems that send Runners to the surface.

Several central figures in the JunkRats narrative operate within or are affected by the system established under his leadership:

  • Adrian
  • Lucy
  • Gabriel
  • Zariah
Story Spoiler

Later in the campaign it is revealed that Zariah is Vince's daughter. Her relationship to the Underground leader was never publicly acknowledged within the settlement.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Adrian
  • Lucy
  • Gabriel

docs.title: Zariah
docs.text: Zariah Profile Role Weapons Specialist of the JunkRats Occupation Surface scavenger Affiliation The Underground Personal Age 20 Gender Female Status Alive Additional Information Voice Actor N/A

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Zariah is one of the central characters in the JunkRats narrative and a member of the group later known as the JunkRats.

She operates on The Surface as a scavenger and weapons specialist, frequently assisting the group during encounters with Forsaken creatures and other threats found throughout the ruins of the former city.

docs.title: Background
docs.text:
Zariah was raised within The Underground, the crater settlement formed after the devastation of The Collapse and the spread of the Forsaken Virus.

Unlike many survivors living inside the settlement, Zariah eventually chose to leave the Underground and survive independently on the surface. There she sustained herself by scavenging materials from the ruined city and constructing or modifying weapons from salvaged components.

Her experience surviving outside the crater allowed her to develop strong familiarity with firearms and improvised weapon systems.

docs.title: Story
docs.text:
Zariah is a major participant in the events of the JunkRats campaign and serves as the group's primary weapons specialist.

Zariah first encountered Adrian, Lucy, and Gabriel inside a partially collapsed building in Dead Man’s Land while working on a modified rifle assembly. After a brief confrontation and discussion, the group invited her to relocate to their safehouse in Dead Man’s Land.

Zariah accepted and began assisting the team by repairing and improving their weapons and combat equipment. With her addition, the four began operating together regularly on the surface.

As their activities continued outside the direct authority of The Underground, the group eventually attracted the attention of Underground scouts. Zariah, along with Adrian, Lucy, and Gabriel, was arrested and charged with treason for diverting resources and conducting unauthorized operations on the surface.

The four were transported to an abandoned subway platform used for executions. Before the sentence could be carried out, structural damage caused by Forsaken growth destabilizing the surrounding tunnels triggered a collapse that destroyed part of the platform. The collapse allowed the group to escape and return to the surface.

[Spoiler]: Late Story Revelation

Later in the campaign it is revealed that Zariah is the daughter of Vince, the leader of The Underground. Her connection to Vince had never been publicly acknowledged within the settlement, and most citizens were unaware the relationship existed.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Zariah is known for her direct and confrontational approach to dangerous situations. She frequently responds quickly to threats and is often among the first to engage hostile targets during encounters on the surface.

Her aggressive approach to combat and her familiarity with weapon systems make her an effective fighter during expeditions outside the crater settlement.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
Zariah regularly operates alongside several individuals:

  • Adrian
  • Lucy
  • Gabriel

Together they form the group later known as the JunkRats. Their relationship develops through repeated surface expeditions and their shared conflict with the authority of The Underground.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Adrian
  • Lucy
  • Gabriel
  • Vince

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This section provides a behind-the-scenes look at the development of JunkRats.

Unlike the main lore sections of the wiki, these pages explore the creative and technical process behind the game while avoiding story spoilers.

Project Overview

The origins and vision behind JunkRats.

docs.title: Project Overview
docs.text:
JunkRats is a story-driven action shooter set in a ruined world where humanity survives beneath the remains of collapsed cities. The game blends grounded combat, exploration, and environmental storytelling to create a tense experience in a world shaped by the spread of the Forsaken Virus.

The project focuses on delivering a cinematic campaign that explores survival, loyalty, and the consequences of human decisions during a global catastrophe. Rather than relying solely on traditional storytelling methods, JunkRats emphasizes narrative discovery through the environments players explore and the characters they encounter.

docs.title: Vision
docs.text:
The goal of JunkRats is to create a world that feels believable, dangerous, and lived in. The ruined city above the survivors is not simply a backdrop for combat, but an active part of the narrative. Abandoned infrastructure, collapsed structures, and forgotten facilities all contribute to the story of how the world fell apart.

Players take control of a small group of survivors who eventually become known as the JunkRats, navigating hostile territory while confronting the growing threat posed by the Forsaken.

The game aims to combine the pacing of modern action shooters with the tension and atmosphere often found in survival horror experiences.

docs.title: Narrative Focus
docs.text:
The JunkRats story centers around a small team of survivors forced to operate outside the control of The Underground. Their journey takes them through dangerous ruins, forgotten research facilities, and unstable areas of the city that have long been abandoned.

Throughout the campaign, players gradually uncover the truth behind the outbreak and the events that shaped the world they now inhabit.

docs.title: Development Philosophy
docs.text:
The development of JunkRats prioritizes several core principles:

  • Atmospheric storytelling through environments and world design
  • Character-driven narrative focused on a small group of survivors
  • Grounded but fast-paced combat that rewards positioning and awareness
  • Exploration of ruined environments that reveal the history of the collapse

These elements work together to create a world that feels consistent, immersive, and reactive to the player’s actions.

docs.title: Factions
docs.text:
The Factions section documents organized groups that operate within the world of JunkRats.

Following The Collapse and the spread of the Forsaken Virus, surviving humans formed small communities and groups in order to survive within the ruined world. Some of these groups operate as structured settlements, while others function as smaller teams working independently on the surface.

These articles describe the groups that influence events within the JunkRats setting.

docs.title: Major Factions
docs.text:
The Underground

The primary human settlement built inside the massive crater created during the Collapse, serving as humanity's largest organized refuge.

The Runners

Scavengers sent from the Underground to the surface to recover scrap, technology, and other resources from the ruins of the old world.

JunkRats

A small group of independent survivors who operate on the surface after separating from the Underground.

docs.title: Faction Types
docs.text:
Groups within the JunkRats setting generally fall into broad categories.

Settlements Organized survivor communities that maintain infrastructure and population centers after The Collapse.

docs.title: JunkRats
docs.text:
The JunkRats are a small group of surface survivors composed of Adrian, Lucy, Gabriel, and Zariah.

The group consists primarily of former Runners who once worked for The Underground. After separating from the settlement, they began operating independently across the ruins of the Surface.

The name JunkRats originated as an insult used by people within the Underground to describe scavengers who spent their lives searching through the debris of the old world. Over time, the group adopted the term themselves.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The JunkRats operate throughout the ruined city surrounding the crater settlement.

Using their experience as former Runners, they navigate abandoned infrastructure, collapsed streets, and dangerous urban environments in search of supplies and shelter. Their familiarity with the surface allows them to travel through areas most Underground patrols avoid.

Because they no longer operate under the authority of the Underground, the group functions independently and survives using whatever resources they are able to recover from the ruins.

docs.title: Origin of the Name
docs.text:
The term “JunkRat” began as a derogatory nickname used by citizens of The Underground.

Runners were often viewed as expendable scavengers who spent their time digging through scrap piles, abandoned buildings, and ruined machinery left behind after The Collapse. As a result, many people began referring to them mockingly as rats digging through junk.

The group later adopted the name themselves, turning what was originally meant as an insult into a symbol of their independence.

docs.title: Activities
docs.text:
The JunkRats primarily survive through scavenging and exploration of the surrounding city ruins.

Typical activities include:

  • Searching abandoned structures for usable equipment
  • Recovering scrap materials and tools
  • Navigating collapsed infrastructure
  • Avoiding or defending against Forsaken encounters

The ruined city environment remains extremely dangerous, with unstable buildings, blocked streets, and frequent Forsaken activity making travel unpredictable.

docs.title: Members
docs.text:
The core members of the JunkRats include:

  • Adrian
  • Lucy
  • Gabriel
  • Zariah

Zariah was never formally assigned to the Runner program within The Underground. Despite this, she worked alongside Adrian, Lucy, and Gabriel during surface expeditions and eventually became part of the group.

docs.title: Relationship with the Underground
docs.text:
The JunkRats are no longer affiliated with The Underground.

Their activities on the surface and separation from the settlement place them outside the authority of its leadership, and interactions between the group and Underground patrols are often hostile.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Adrian
  • Lucy
  • Gabriel
  • Zariah

docs.title: Runners
docs.text:
Runners are scavengers sent from The Underground to the Surface to recover resources from the ruins of the old world.

Following The Collapse, the crater settlement became dependent on materials recovered from the surrounding city. Without a steady supply of scrap, equipment, and technology, the Underground would not be able to maintain its infrastructure or support its population.

Runners perform the dangerous task of retrieving these resources from the abandoned city.

Within the Underground, Runners are sometimes mockingly referred to as “JunkRats.” The term comes from the perception that they spend their lives digging through the junk and ruins of the old world like rats searching for scraps. While originally used as an insult, the name was later adopted by the group formed by Adrian, Lucy, Gabriel, and Zariah.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
The primary role of Runners is to recover materials required to sustain life within the Underground.

These resources include tools, machinery, medical supplies, electronic components, and other usable equipment left behind after civilization fell. Salvaged materials are used to repair infrastructure, maintain power systems, and construct new structures inside the crater settlement.

Without the work of Runners, the Underground would eventually exhaust the resources necessary for survival.

docs.title: Recruitment
docs.text:
Runners are selected through a lottery system within the Underground.

Citizens whose names are drawn are assigned to Runner duty and sent to the surface to participate in resource recovery missions. Due to the extremely high mortality rate, many residents view selection as a likely death sentence.

Despite the danger, the system continues to operate because the Underground relies heavily on the resources recovered during these expeditions.

docs.title: Surface Operations
docs.text:
Runners travel through the ruined city in search of usable materials.

The environment outside the crater is extremely hazardous. Buildings are unstable, entire streets have collapsed, and large portions of the city are inhabited by Forsaken creatures created by the Forsaken Virus.

Because of these conditions, every expedition carries a significant risk.

docs.title: Risk
docs.text:
Many Runners never return from the surface.

Encounters with the Forsaken, collapsing structures, and environmental hazards make each mission unpredictable. Despite this, the role remains essential to the survival of the Underground.

For this reason, Runner duty is considered one of the most dangerous responsibilities within the settlement.

docs.title: Known Runners
docs.text:
Notable individuals who have served as Runners include:

  • Adrian
  • Lucy
  • Gabriel

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Adrian
  • Lucy
  • Gabriel
  • Zariah

docs.title: The Underground
docs.text:
The Underground is the largest organized human settlement in the world of JunkRats and the primary center of human survival following The Collapse.

The faction operates from within a massive crater created when part of the city collapsed during early attempts to contain the spread of the Forsaken Virus. Over time, survivors gathered inside the crater and rebuilt the area into a functioning settlement using materials salvaged from the surrounding ruins.

Today, the Underground functions as both a settlement and a governing authority over the surviving population that lives within the crater.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
The Underground operates as a highly controlled survival community.

Leadership is centralized under Vince, who oversees the stability and operation of the settlement. Infrastructure maintenance, resource management, and security are tightly organized in order to preserve the limited resources available to the population.

Because the world outside the crater remains extremely dangerous, most residents rarely leave the settlement.

docs.title: Territory
docs.text:
The Underground occupies the interior of a collapsed city district.

The crater lies below the level of the surrounding streets, with the ruined remains of buildings forming a ring around the settlement. Structures within the Underground are built along the inner slopes of the crater and across the lower sections of the collapse zone.

Although the crater sits below street level, it remains open to the sky and is partially connected to surrounding infrastructure such as tunnels and underground transit systems.

docs.title: Surface Operations
docs.text:
Despite its relative safety, the Underground cannot sustain itself without resources recovered from the ruined city above.

To obtain these materials, the faction organizes specialized scavenging teams known as Runners. These individuals are sent to the Surface to recover scrap, equipment, technology, and other supplies required to maintain the settlement.

Runner missions are considered some of the most dangerous assignments within the Underground due to the presence of the Forsaken and the unstable condition of the abandoned city.

docs.title: Influence
docs.text:
As the largest surviving human settlement, the Underground exerts significant influence over the remaining population in the region.

Its systems of leadership, supply control, and surface operations allow the settlement to maintain a fragile stability in a world largely overrun by infection.

However, not all survivors operate within the authority of the Underground, and some individuals have begun operating independently in the ruins beyond the crater.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Vince
  • Runners

docs.title: Lore Overview
docs.text:
The JunkRats setting takes place after The Collapse, a global event caused by the spread of the Forsaken Virus.

Most human civilization was destroyed during the outbreak. The largest surviving population now lives inside a massive crater formed when part of a city collapsed during early attempts to stop the infection. Over time, survivors rebuilt within the crater and established a settlement known as The Underground.

The crater lies below the surrounding city streets but remains open to the sky. Buildings and infrastructure from the former city remain along its outer edges, while the settlement occupies the slopes and lower sections of the collapse zone.

This section contains reference articles describing the world, history, locations, and major elements of the JunkRats setting.

docs.title: World and History
docs.text:
World Overview

Overview of the JunkRats setting and the condition of the world after the Collapse.

The Collapse

The global event that led to the fall of human civilization.

The Forsaken Virus

The infection responsible for the mutation of living organisms.

docs.title: Survivors
docs.text:
The Underground

The primary human settlement located inside the crater.

Runners

Individuals sent to the surface to recover resources for the Underground.

docs.title: Creatures
docs.text:
Forsaken

Mutated organisms created by infection from the Forsaken Virus.

docs.title: Locations
docs.text:
Dead Man's Land

A region of ruined city located outside the crater.

The Surface

The abandoned urban environment surrounding the crater settlement.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text: • The Underground

docs.title: Dead Man's Land
docs.text:
Dead Man's Land Classification Type Region Status Accessible Location Region The Surface Position Outside the crater

Dead Man's Land is a region of ruined city located several miles from The Underground.

The area is part of the surrounding urban ruins left behind after The Collapse. It consists of abandoned buildings, damaged infrastructure, and fractured streets from the former city district.

docs.title: Location
docs.text:
Dead Man's Land lies outside the crater that contains the Underground settlement.

The region forms part of the wider Surface environment surrounding the crater and connects to deeper sections of the ruined city.

docs.title: Forsaken Presence
docs.text:
Compared to many other areas of the ruined city, Forsaken activity in Dead Man's Land is relatively limited.

Forsaken creatures are known to pass through the region, but they do not appear to remain there for extended periods. The reason for this behavior is unknown.

docs.title: Surface Access
docs.text:
Dead Man's Land is one of the outer regions encountered by Runners during surface operations beyond the crater settlement.

Due to its proximity to the Underground, the area forms part of the surrounding environment used during resource recovery missions.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• The Underground
  • Runners

docs.title: The Forsaken Virus
docs.text:
Forsaken Virus Classification Type Biological agent Origin Forsaken Virus Status Active Characteristics Habitat The Surface Atmosphere

The Forsaken Virus is the mutating infection responsible for the collapse of human civilization in the world of JunkRats.

The virus spreads through organic life and causes extreme biological mutation. Infected organisms are transformed into hostile creatures collectively known as the Forsaken.

The rapid spread of the virus led directly to the global collapse known as The Collapse.

docs.title: Infection
docs.text:
The Forsaken virus spreads through direct exposure to infected biological matter and contaminated environments.

Once infected, the virus rapidly begins altering the host’s body. Tissue, bone, and internal systems mutate as the infection spreads through the organism. These mutations eventually transform the host into a hostile creature.

The infection affects nearly all forms of organic life, making containment extremely difficult.

docs.title: Mutation
docs.text:
Victims of the virus do not remain biologically stable. The infection continues mutating the host over time, reshaping the body into increasingly aggressive and hostile forms.

These infected organisms are collectively referred to as the Forsaken.

Forsaken creatures are known for their aggressive behavior and their ability to survive in environments that would normally be fatal to uninfected organisms.

docs.title: Environmental Spread
docs.text:
The virus does not remain limited to living hosts.

Over time, infected growths and tendrils begin spreading across surrounding structures and environments. These growths can appear along buildings, streets, tunnels, and other surfaces within infected areas.

This environmental spread allows the infection to continue expanding even in locations where living hosts are no longer present.

docs.title: Atmospheric Effects
docs.text:
As the infection continued spreading across large regions of the world, dense concentrations of the virus began accumulating in the atmosphere.

These infected clouds reduced the amount of sunlight reaching the surface, leaving much of the world in a dim twilight environment.

docs.title: Role in the Collapse
docs.text:
The spread of the Forsaken virus led directly to the global event known as The Collapse.

As the infection spread across cities and living populations, civilization rapidly fell apart. Infrastructure failed, large areas were abandoned, and much of the world became overrun by infected organisms.

The resulting collapse of society marked the beginning of the post-collapse world depicted in JunkRats.

docs.title: Forsaken
docs.text:
Forsaken Classification Type Mutated organism Origin Forsaken Virus Status Active Characteristics Habitat The Surface Behavior Hostile

The Forsaken are mutated organisms created by infection from the Forsaken Virus.

When living organisms become infected, the virus rapidly alters their bodies, transforming them into aggressive and hostile creatures. These infected beings are collectively referred to as the Forsaken.

The spread of the Forsaken played a major role in the global event known as The Collapse.

docs.title: Origin
docs.text:
The Forsaken are created when a living organism becomes infected by the Forsaken Virus.

Once infected, the virus begins rapidly mutating the host's biological structure. The infection spreads throughout the body, altering tissue and bone while reshaping the organism into a new form.

The final result is a hostile creature driven by instinct rather than normal behavior.

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
Forsaken are known for their aggressive and unpredictable behavior.

They commonly inhabit the ruins of the surface world and are frequently encountered throughout abandoned sections of the city. Their presence makes much of the surface extremely dangerous for survivors.

Forsaken activity is one of the primary threats faced by Runners during surface operations.

docs.title: Environment
docs.text:
Forsaken are most commonly found in the ruins of the old world following The Collapse.

Infected growths linked to the virus can often be seen spreading across buildings, streets, and other surfaces within these areas. These environments are often heavily contaminated by the infection.

docs.title: Relation to Survivors
docs.text:
Surviving human populations largely remain inside The Underground to avoid encounters with the Forsaken.

Travel to the surface is limited primarily to organized scavenging missions carried out by Runners.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Forsaken Virus
  • Runners
  • The Underground

docs.title: The Surface
docs.text:
The Surface Classification Type Ruined urban environment Status Uncontrolled Location Region Outside the crater Position Above The Underground Additional Information Affiliation Forsaken Population
  • Scattered human survivors
  • Large Forsaken presence

The Surface refers to the remains of the world above the crater settlement known as The Underground.

Following The Collapse, most of the planet’s cities were abandoned as the Forsaken Virus spread across populated regions. The environment above ground now consists of ruined urban districts, damaged infrastructure, and large areas of uncontrolled infection.

The Surface contains the remains of the pre-collapse world, including buildings, streets, vehicles, and industrial facilities left behind when civilization fell.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
The Surface is composed primarily of abandoned city environments and surrounding infrastructure.

Buildings remain partially standing in many areas, though structural damage and long-term decay have left large sections unstable. Roads are fractured, debris fields cover large parts of the city, and many districts show signs of extensive collapse.

Remnants of the Forsaken Virus can be found throughout the environment. Infected growths, tendrils, and contamination are present across many surfaces and structures.

docs.title: Forsaken Presence
docs.text:
Large portions of the Surface are inhabited by Forsaken creatures created by infection from the Forsaken Virus.

These organisms move through abandoned districts and ruined structures across the city. Their presence makes most areas of the Surface dangerous for unprotected survivors.

docs.title: Surface Operations
docs.text:
Despite the danger, the Surface remains an important source of resources for the survivors living in The Underground.

Teams known as Runners travel into the ruins to recover materials left behind after the collapse of civilization. Salvaged equipment, machinery, and tools are used to maintain infrastructure within the crater settlement.

Surface operations are considered high-risk due to environmental hazards and encounters with the Forsaken.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• The Underground
  • Forsaken
  • Forsaken Virus
  • Runners

docs.title: The Collapse
docs.text:
The Collapse refers to the global fall of human civilization following the spread of the Forsaken virus.

The outbreak spread rapidly across populated regions, infecting nearly all forms of organic life. As the infection expanded through cities and infrastructure, governments collapsed and large sections of the world were abandoned.

docs.title: Spread of the Virus
docs.text:
During the early stages of the outbreak, the virus moved quickly through major population centers.

As the infection progressed, black and purple tendrils began spreading across buildings, streets, and surrounding environments. Entire districts became overrun as the infection continued expanding.

Individuals infected by the virus did not simply die. Their bodies mutated into hostile organisms later known as the Forsaken.

docs.title: Failed Bombing Attempts
docs.text:
As containment efforts failed, authorities attempted to slow the spread by bombing heavily infected urban areas.

By this stage the virus had already begun to evolve. Aircraft instruments and navigation systems frequently failed when approaching infected zones, and radio signals became unreliable. Only a limited number of bombs successfully reached the inner city before the operation was abandoned.

One of these blasts destroyed a large section of the city center, collapsing buildings and streets and creating a massive crater.

docs.title: Atmospheric Changes
docs.text:
As the infection spread, dense concentrations of the virus began accumulating in the atmosphere.

These infected clouds reduced the amount of sunlight reaching the surface, leaving many regions in a dim twilight environment.

docs.title: Aftermath
docs.text:
Following the collapse of global infrastructure, survivors sought shelter wherever they could.

One of the most significant survivor settlements eventually formed inside the massive crater left behind by the bombing. This settlement later became known as The Underground.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Forsaken virus
  • Forsaken
  • The Underground

docs.title: World Overview
docs.text:
JunkRats takes place in a ruined world shaped by infection, collapse, and the struggle to survive within the remains of a fallen city.

The events of JunkRats occur years after The Collapse of civilization caused by a mutating infection known as the Forsaken Virus.

When the outbreak spread, it rapidly consumed cities and corrupted nearly all living organisms. Governments collapsed, evacuation efforts failed, and large regions of the world were abandoned. Over time the remains of civilization decayed into fractured districts, unstable structures, and empty infrastructure left to rot.

Across the ruins, infected creatures known as the Forsaken roam freely through collapsed buildings and broken streets. Their presence has transformed much of the surface into a hostile wasteland where survival is rare.

Humanity did not vanish entirely, but most of it was wiped out. The largest remaining population now lives inside a massive crater formed when part of the city collapsed during early attempts to contain the outbreak.

docs.title: Life After the Collapse
docs.text:
The surface world is unstable and dangerous.

Years of structural failure have left buildings weakened and entire districts fractured. Streets have sunk, structures lean at dangerous angles, and parts of the ruined city continue to collapse without warning. In some areas the infection has spread through the environment itself, overtaking concrete, tunnels, and forgotten infrastructure.

Despite these dangers, the surface still holds valuable remnants of the old world. Scrap, machinery, tools, and medical supplies remain scattered throughout the ruins. Recovering these resources is essential for keeping the surviving population alive.

docs.title: The Underground
docs.text:
The largest surviving settlement is known as The Underground

The settlement was built inside a massive crater created during the early days of the outbreak when an attempt to destroy infected sections of the city caused part of the district to collapse. Survivors gradually rebuilt within the crater, forming a structured community inside the exposed collapse zone of the ruined city.

Homes, workshops, walkways, and defensive structures were assembled from salvaged materials taken from the surrounding ruins. Over time the crater developed into a functioning settlement supported by recovered power systems, filtration equipment, and reinforced infrastructure.

Life inside the Underground offers stability compared to the surface, but it is heavily regulated. Resources are tightly controlled, movement is monitored, and survival depends on maintaining the systems that keep the settlement operating.

docs.title: Runners
docs.text:
To keep the settlement alive, resources must still be recovered from the surface.

This task falls to scavengers known as Runners. Runners are sent into the ruins to retrieve scrap, equipment, and other supplies needed to sustain the crater settlement.

These expeditions are extremely dangerous. Forsaken activity, unstable buildings, and hostile environments make every run a risk. Many Runners never return.

Despite the danger, the role is essential. The materials brought back from the surface allow the Underground to repair infrastructure, maintain power, and continue supporting the last major population of survivors.

docs.text:
